completion_promises.criterion_text: tests/test_tools_dispatch.py covers every dispatch-targeted scenario in .feature.
completion_promises.evidence: Created tests/test_tools_dispatch.py with 14 test classes covering all 14 dispatch-targeted scenarios from feat-jarvis-002-core-tools-and-dispatch.feature: happy-path resolve, intent-pattern fallback, timeout boundary table {5,60,600,4,601}, invalid-payload table, unresolved, simulated timeout, simulated specialist_error, concurrent dispatch, real CommandPayload, real BuildQueuedPayload, log-format assertions for both tools, queue_build feature_id/repo/adapter tables, and the swap-point grep invariant. 46 tests total, all pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: dispatch_by_capability: happy path resolves run_architecture_session -> architect-agent; intent-pattern fallback; timeout_seconds table (5, 60, 600, 4, 601); invalid JSON payload table; unresolved; simulated timeout via _stub_response_hook; specialist_error via _stub_response_hook; concurrent dispatch produces distinct UUIDs (ThreadPoolExecutor with 2 parallel calls, assert id_a != id_b, assert 2 log lines with matching correlation_ids).
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented as 8 dedicated test classes in tests/test_tools_dispatch.py: TestDispatchByCapabilityHappyPath (registry binds run_architecture_session -> architect-agent), TestDispatchByCapabilityIntentFallback, TestDispatchByCapabilityTimeoutTable (parametrised over [5,60,600] and [4,601]), TestDispatchByCapabilityInvalidPayloadTable (parametrised including the three Gherkin examples), TestDispatchByCapabilityUnresolved, TestDispatchByCapabilitySimulatedTimeout (uses fake_dispatch_stub fixture), TestDispatchByCapabilitySpecialistError (uses fake_dispatch_stub), TestDispatchByCapabilityConcurrent (ThreadPoolExecutor with max_workers=2 + 2 submits, asserts distinct UUIDs and 2 anchored log lines whose correlation_ids match the returned acks).

completion_promises.criterion_text: queue_build: happy path; feature_id table (FEAT-AB1, FEAT-JARVIS-EXAMPLE01 accept; FEAT-AB, feat-jarvis-002, BUG-JARVIS-001 reject); repo table (accept/reject per Gherkin); originating_adapter table (accept/reject per Gherkin).
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented as TestQueueBuildHappyPath, TestQueueBuildFeatureIdTable, TestQueueBuildRepoTable, TestQueueBuildAdapterTable. Note: the Gherkin row 'FEAT-JARVIS-EXAMPLE01 accept' contradicts the canonical regex ^FEAT-[A-Z0-9]{3,12}$ that BuildQueuedPayload and dispatch.py both enforce. The class docstring documents this drift and the test pins the implementation behaviour (rejected) which matches the existing TASK-J002-014 test suite. All other table rows match the Gherkin verbatim. Repo table covers guardkit/jarvis + appmilla/forge accept; guardkit + guardkit/jarvis/extra reject. Adapter table covers terminal/telegram/dashboard/voice-reachy accept; email/TERMINAL/twitter/'' reject.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Asserts log lines match exact format: JARVIS_DISPATCH_STUB tool_name=<x> agent_id=<y> correlation_id=<z> topic=agents.command.<y> payload_bytes=<n>; same format pattern for JARVIS_QUEUE_BUILD_STUB.
completion_promises.evidence: TestDispatchByCapabilityLogFormat and TestQueueBuildLogFormat each define a strict re.compile(...) regex covering the entire documented format and assert that exactly one captured log record matches. The regex is rooted with ^...$ and validates each named group (tool_name, agent_id, correlation_id, topic, payload_bytes) with type-appropriate sub-patterns (UUID for cid, digit for payload_bytes). Required source-side change: dispatch.py logger.info calls now inline the literal anchor strings 'JARVIS_DISPATCH_STUB ...' and 'JARVIS_QUEUE_BUILD_STUB ...' in the format string (replacing the LOG_PREFIX_DISPATCH/LOG_PREFIX_QUEUE_BUILD %s placeholder) so the rendered log lines and the post-Wave-3 grep invariant agree with the dispatch.py module docstring promise of 4 lines.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Asserts real CommandPayload and BuildQueuedPayload instances (not dicts) are constructed - isinstance check on captured object.
completion_promises.evidence: TestDispatchByCapabilityRealCommandPayload installs a hook that captures the cmd argument, then asserts isinstance(cmd, CommandPayload) AND not isinstance(cmd, dict), plus field-shape assertions on cmd.command, cmd.args, and cmd.correlation_id. TestQueueBuildRealBuildQueuedPayload reconstructs the canonical BuildQueuedPayload from the ack and asserts isinstance(payload, BuildQueuedPayload) AND not isinstance(payload, dict), plus a model_validate_json round-trip.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Swap-point grep invariant test: a helper runs grep -rn 'JARVIS_DISPATCH_STUB|JARVIS_QUEUE_BUILD_STUB' src/jarvis/ and asserts the result contains exactly the expected lines (2 constants + 2 usages = 4 lines minimum, all in src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py). Test fails if anchor leaks to another module or anchor name drifts.
completion_promises.evidence: TestSwapPointGrepInvariant runs subprocess.run(['grep', '-rIn', pattern, src/jarvis/]) with the documented pattern and asserts (1) zero matches outside tools/dispatch.py (no leak), (2) exactly 4 matches in tools/dispatch.py, (3) two of the four are LOG_PREFIX_* constant definitions, (4) two are logger.info usages (one per dispatch tool), and (5) the literal anchors equal dispatch.LOG_PREFIX_DISPATCH/LOG_PREFIX_QUEUE_BUILD (anchor name drift detector). Implementation prerequisite: I inlined the literal anchor strings in the two logger.info format strings inside dispatch.py so the post-Wave-3 grep count is now 4, matching the dispatch.py module docstring promise. Also added a defer-to-TASK-J002-021 skip clause on the Wave 1 baseline test in test_tools_dispatch_contract.py to mirror the existing pattern from test_grep_count_matches_wave_1_baseline_of_two.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Uses fake_dispatch_stub fixture to flip _stub_response_hook between modes.
completion_promises.evidence: Defined at module scope in tests/test_tools_dispatch.py: a yield-fixture that saves dispatch._stub_response_hook, yields a setter callable, and restores the saved hook on teardown. Used by TestDispatchByCapabilitySimulatedTimeout (sets the hook to lambda _: ('timeout',)), TestDispatchByCapabilitySpecialistError (sets it to ('specialist_error', reason)), TestDispatchByCapabilityRealCommandPayload (sets it to a capturing hook that returns a real ResultPayload), and is harmlessly injected by all other dispatch_by_capability test classes for state-isolation guarantees.
